title: Sarga 22
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, mind and meditation. The passage moves from “Having heard that great roar, Lord Yama considered the enemy victorious and his own army destroyed.” through “Then, having seen Time thus enraged and terrifying to all worlds, the three worlds were agitated and the gods trembled.” through “But Ravana, upon seeing that chariot which struck terror into the world, was neither disturbed nor did fear enter his heart.”

Themes: devotion, mortality and immortality. The passage moves from “Yama, greatly enraged, approached Ravana and hurled spears and javelins, piercing Ravana's vital spots.” through “Then, the battle between the chief of the Rākṣasas and the lord of the departed spirits became like the cosmic dissolution of the worlds.” through “Then, Death, even more enraged, spoke to Yama, saying, "Release me in battle so that I may kill this sinful demon, for by nature, this demon…”

Themes: devotion, virtue and right conduct, mortality and immortality. The passage moves from “The glorious Hiranyakashipu, Namuchi, Shambara, Visandhi, Dhumaketu, and Bali Vairochana were among them.” through “This is not merely my strength, but a natural rule that anyone seen by me does not live even for a moment.” through “That great weapon, surrounded by flames as if burning the demon, trembled when touched by the powerful Yama.”

Themes: devotion, virtue and right conduct, mortality and immortality. The passage moves from “Then all those on the battlefield, terrified by that, fled, and all the gods were agitated upon seeing Yama with his mace raised.” through “Indeed, this unfailing staff of Time, of immeasurable power for all living beings, was created by me long ago, with Death as its foremost aspect.” through “What indeed can I do now, having come to battle, if this one (Ravana) cannot be killed by me because he is protected by a boon?”

Themes: devotion. The passage moves from “Having said, "Therefore, I disappear from the sight of this demon," he vanished right there with his chariot and horses.” through “Dashagriva, having conquered him and proclaimed his own name, ascended the Pushpaka chariot again and departed from Yama's abode.” through “And Vaivasvata (Yama), delighted, went to heaven along with the gods led by Brahma, as did the great sage Narada. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे…”

verses.sanskrit: स तस्य तु महानादं श्रुत्वा वैवस्वतः प्रभुः । शत्रुं विजयिनं मेने स्वबलस्य च सङ्क्षयम् ।। 7.22.1 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard that great roar, Lord Yama considered the enemy victorious and his own army destroyed.

verses.sanskrit: स हि योधान्हतान्मत्वा क्रोधसंरक्तलोचनः । अब्रवीत्त्वरितं सूतं रथो ऽयमुपनीयताम् ।। 7.22.2 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, he (Yama), considering his warriors killed and his eyes reddened with anger, quickly told his charioteer, "Bring this chariot near."

verses.sanskrit: तस्य सूतस्तदा दिव्यमुपस्थाप्य महारथम् । स्थितः स च महातेजा ह्यध्यारोहत तं रथम् । प्राशमुद्गरहस्तश्च मृत्युस्तस्याग्रतः स्थितः ।। 7.22.3 ।।
verses.translation: Then, his charioteer, having brought near the divine great chariot, stood ready, and Yama, of great splendor, indeed ascended that chariot, while Death, holding a spear and mace, stood before him.

verses.sanskrit: येन सङ्क्षिप्यते सर्वं त्रैलोक्यमिदमव्ययम् । कालदण्डस्तु पार्श्वस्थो मूर्तिमानस्य चाभवत् । यमप्रहरणं दिव्यं तेजसा ज्वलदग्निमत् ।। 7.22.4 ।।
verses.translation: The imperishable three worlds are brought to an end by him, and the staff of Time, Yama's divine, blazing, fiery weapon, appeared personified by his side.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य पार्श्वेषु निच्छिद्राः कालपाशाः प्रतिष्ठिताः ।। 7.22.5 ।।
verses.translation: On his sides were placed the inescapable nooses of Time.

verses.sanskrit: पावकस्पर्शसङ्काशः स्थितो मूर्तश्च मुद्गरः ।। 7.22.6 ।।
verses.translation: An embodied mace, whose touch was like fire, also stood there.

verses.sanskrit: ततो लोकत्रयं क्षुब्धमकम्पन्त दिवौकसः । कालं दृष्ट्वा तथा क्रुद्धं सर्वलोकभयावहम् ।। 7.22.7 ।।
verses.translation: Then, having seen Time thus enraged and terrifying to all worlds, the three worlds were agitated and the gods trembled.

verses.sanskrit: ततः प्रचोदयन्सूतस्तानश्वान्रुचिरप्रभान् । प्रययौ भीमसन्नादो यत्र रक्षःपतिः स्थितः ।। 7.22.8 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the charioteer, urging those brightly shining horses, proceeded with a terrible roar to where the lord of Rakshasas was situated.

verses.sanskrit: मुहूर्तेन यमं ते तु हया हरिहयोपमाः । प्रापयन्मनसस्तुल्या यत्र तत्प्रस्तुतं रणम् ।। 7.22.9 ।।
verses.translation: In an instant, those horses, swift as thought and resembling Indra's steeds, brought Yama to the place where the battle had begun.

verses.sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तथैव विकृतं रथं मृत्युसमन्वितम् । सचिवा राक्षसेन्द्रस्य सहसा विप्रदुद्रुवुः ।। 7.22.10 ।।
verses.translation: Upon seeing that terrifying chariot, which seemed to embody death itself, the ministers of Ravana immediately fled in every direction.

verses.sanskrit: लघुसत्त्वतया ते हि नष्टसञ्ज्ञा भयार्दिताः । नेह योद्धुं समर्थाः स्म इत्युक्त्वा प्रययुर्दिशः ।। 7.22.11 ।।
verses.translation: Overwhelmed by fear and having lost their senses due to their weak courage, they declared, "We are not capable of fighting here," and fled in all directions.

verses.sanskrit: स तु तं तादृशं दृष्ट्वा रथं लोकभयावहम् । नाक्षुभ्यत दशग्रीवो न चापि भयमाविशत् ।। 7.22.12 ।।
verses.translation: But Ravana, upon seeing that chariot which struck terror into the world, was neither disturbed nor did fear enter his heart.

verses.sanskrit: स तु रावणमासाद्य व्यसृजच्छक्तितोमरान् । यमो मर्माणि सङ्क्रुद्धो रावणस्योपकृन्तत ।। 7.22.13 ।।
verses.translation: Yama, greatly enraged, approached Ravana and hurled spears and javelins, piercing Ravana's vital spots.

verses.sanskrit: रावणस्तु ततः स्वस्थः शरवर्षं मुमोच ह । तस्मिन्वैवस्वतरथे तोयवर्षमिवाम्बुदः ।। 7.22.14 ।।
verses.translation: Ravana, having regained his composure, then released a shower of arrows upon Yama's chariot, much like a cloud pouring down rain.

verses.sanskrit: ततो महाशक्तिशरैः पात्यमानैर्महोरसि (पात्यमानो) । नाशक्नोत्प्रतिकर्तुं स राक्षसः शल्यपीडितः ।। 7.22.15 ।।
verses.translation: Then, as powerful arrows struck his broad chest, that demon, afflicted by the shafts, was unable to retaliate.

verses.sanskrit: एवं नानाप्रहरणैर्यमेनामित्रकर्षिणा । सप्तरात्रं कृतः सङ्ख्ये विसञ्ज्ञो विमुखो रिपुः ।। 7.22.16 ।।
verses.translation: Thus, for seven nights in battle, the enemy Ravana was rendered senseless and turned away by Yama, the tormentor of foes, using various weapons.

verses.sanskrit: तदासीत्तुमुलं युद्धं यमराक्षसयोर्द्वयोः । जयमाकाङ्क्षतोर्वीर समरेष्वनिवर्तिनोः ।। 7.22.17 ।।
verses.translation: Then, a fierce battle ensued between Yama and the demon, both unyielding warriors desiring victory in their fight.

verses.sanskrit: ततो देवाः सगन्धर्वाः सिद्धाश्च परमर्षयः । प्रजापतिं पुरस्कृत्य समेतास्तद्रणाजिरम् ।। 7.22.18 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the gods, along with Gandharvas, Siddhas, and great sages, assembled at that battlefield, led by Prajapati Brahma.

verses.sanskrit: संवर्त इव लोकानां क्रुध्यतोरभवत्तदा । राक्षसानां च मुख्यस्य प्रेतानामीश्वरस्य च ।। 7.22.19 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the battle between the chief of the Rākṣasas and the lord of the departed spirits became like the cosmic dissolution of the worlds.

verses.sanskrit: राक्षसेन्द्रो ऽपि विस्फार्य चापमिन्द्राशनिप्रभम् । निरन्तरमिवाकाशं कुर्वन्बाणांस्ततो ऽसृजत् ।। 7.22.20 ।।
verses.translation: The lord of Rākṣasas, drawing his bow which shone like Indra's thunderbolt, then released arrows, making the sky appear as if it had no gaps.

verses.sanskrit: मृत्युं चतुर्भिर्विशिखैः सूतं सप्तभिरार्दयत् । यमं शतसहस्रेण शीघ्रं मर्मस्वताडयत् ।। 7.22.21 ।।
verses.translation: Rāvaṇa wounded Death with four sharp arrows, afflicted the charioteer with seven, and quickly struck Yama in his vital spots with a hundred thousand arrows.

verses.sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धस्य वदनाद्यमस्य समजायत । ज्वालामाली सनिःश्वासः सधूमः कोपपावकः ।। 7.22.22 ।।
verses.translation: Then, from the enraged Yama's face, a fire of anger arose, garlanded with flames, accompanied by his breath and smoke.

verses.sanskrit: तदाश्चर्यमथो दृष्ट्वा देवदानवसन्निधौ । प्रहर्षितौ सुसंरब्धौ मृत्युकालौ बभूवतुः ।। 7.22.23 ।।
verses.translation: Then, having witnessed that marvel in the presence of gods and Dānavas, Death and Kāla became greatly delighted and exceedingly furious.

verses.sanskrit: ततो मृत्युः क्रुद्धतरो वैवस्वतमभाषत । मुञ्च मां समरे यावद्धन्मीमं पापराक्षसम् । नैषा रक्षो भवेदद्य मर्यादा हि निसर्गतः ।। 7.22.24 ।।
verses.translation: Then, Death, even more enraged, spoke to Yama, saying, "Release me in battle so that I may kill this sinful demon, for by nature, this demon should not exist today as a boundary."

verses.sanskrit: हिरण्यकशिपुः श्रीमान्नमुचिः शम्बरस्तथा । विसन्धिर्धूमकेतुश्च बलिर्वैरोचनो ऽपि च ।। 7.22.25 ।।
verses.translation: The glorious Hiranyakashipu, Namuchi, Shambara, Visandhi, Dhumaketu, and Bali Vairochana were among them.

verses.sanskrit: दम्भुर्दैत्यमहाराजो वृत्रो बाणस्तथैव च । राजर्षयः शास्त्रविदो गन्धर्वाः समहोरगाः ।। 7.22.26 ।।
verses.translation: Dambhu, the great king of Daityas, Vritra, and Bana, along with royal sages who knew the scriptures, Gandharvas, and great serpents, were also present.

verses.sanskrit: ऋषयः पन्नगा दैत्या यक्षाश्चाप्यप्सरोगणाः । युगान्तपरिवर्ते च पृथिवी समहार्णवा ।। 7.22.27 ।।
verses.translation: Sages, serpents, Daityas, Yakshas, and groups of Apsaras, along with the earth and its great oceans at the end of an age, were all present.

verses.sanskrit: क्षयं नीता महाराज सपर्वतसरिद्द्रुमा । एते चान्ये च बहवो बलवन्तो दुरासदाः ।। 7.22.28 ।।
verses.translation: O great king, these and many other powerful and formidable beings, along with mountains, rivers, and trees, were all brought to destruction.

verses.sanskrit: विनिपन्ना मया दृष्टाः किमुतायं निशाचरः ।। 7.22.29 ।।
verses.translation: I have seen many fallen, so what to say of this demon?

verses.sanskrit: मुञ्चं मां साधु धर्मज्ञ यावदेनं निहन्म्यहम् । नहि कश्चिन्मया दृष्टो बलवानपि जीवति ।। 7.22.30 ।।
verses.translation: O knower of dharma, release me so I may kill this one, for no one seen by me, however powerful, has ever lived.

verses.sanskrit: बलं मम न खल्वेतन्मर्यादैषा निसर्गतः । स दृष्टो न मया कालं मुहुर्तमापि जीवति ।। 7.22.31 ।।
verses.translation: This is not merely my strength, but a natural rule that anyone seen by me does not live even for a moment.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यैवं वचनं श्रुत्वा धर्मराजः प्रतापवान् । अब्रवीत्तत्र तं मुत्युं त्वं तिष्ठैनं निहन्म्यहम् ।। 7.22.32 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard his words, the powerful Dharmaraja then said to Death, "You wait, I will kill this one."

verses.sanskrit: ततः संरक्तनयनः क्रुद्धो वैवस्वतः प्रभुः । कालदण्डममोघं तु तोलयामास पाणिना ।। 7.22.33 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the enraged Lord Yama, with blood-red eyes, indeed lifted his unfailing rod of death with his hand.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य पार्श्वेषु निखिला कालपाशाः प्रतिष्ठिताः । पावकाशनिसङ्काशो मुद्गरो मूर्तिमान्स्थितः ।। 7.22.34 ।।
verses.translation: On whose sides all the nooses of Death are established, and an embodied mace, resembling fire and lightning, stands.

verses.sanskrit: दर्शनादेव यः प्राणान्प्राणिनामपकर्षति । किं पुनः स्पृश्यमानस्य पात्यमानस्य वा पुनः ।। 7.22.35 ।।
verses.translation: What more can be said of one who is touched or struck down, when merely by sight it draws away the lives of living beings?

verses.sanskrit: स ज्वालापरिवारस्तु निर्दहन्निव राक्षसम् । तेन स्पृष्टो बलवता महाप्रहरणो ऽस्फुरत् ।। 7.22.36 ।।
verses.translation: That great weapon, surrounded by flames as if burning the demon, trembled when touched by the powerful Yama.

verses.sanskrit: ततो विदुद्रुवुः सर्वे तस्मात्रस्ता रणाजिरे । सुराश्च क्षुभिताः सर्वे दृष्ट्वा दण्डोद्यतं यमम् ।। 7.22.37 ।।
verses.translation: Then all those on the battlefield, terrified by that, fled, and all the gods were agitated upon seeing Yama with his mace raised.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन्प्रहर्तुकामे तु यमे दण्डेन रावणम् । यमं पितामहः साक्षाद्दर्शयित्वेदमब्रवीत् ।। 7.22.38 ।।
verses.translation: When Yama was indeed desirous of striking Ravana with his mace, the grandfather Brahma directly appeared before Yama and spoke these words.

verses.sanskrit: वैवस्वत महाबाहो न खल्वमितविक्रम । न हन्तव्यस्त्वया तेन दण्डेनैव निशाचरः ।। 7.22.39 ।।
verses.translation: O mighty-armed Yama, son of Vivasvat, you of immeasurable prowess, indeed that demon should not be killed by your staff alone.

verses.sanskrit: वरः खलु मयैतस्मै दत्तस्त्रिदशपुङ्गव । स त्वया नानृतः कार्यो यन्मया व्याहृतं वचः ।। 7.22.40 ।।
verses.translation: O chief among gods, indeed a boon has been given by me to him, and that word spoken by me should not be made false by you.

verses.sanskrit: यो हि मामनृतं कुर्याद्देवो वा मानुषो ऽपि वा । त्रैलोक्यमनृतं तेन कृतं स्यान्नात्र संशयः ।। 7.22.41 ।।
verses.translation: Whoever, be it a god or even a human, would make my word false, by him the three worlds would indeed be made false, there is no doubt about this.

verses.sanskrit: क्रुद्धेन विप्रमुक्तो ऽयं निर्विशेषं प्रियाप्रिये । प्रजाः संहरते रौद्रो लोकत्रयभयावहः ।। 7.22.42 ।।
verses.translation: This terrible staff, when released by an angry one, destroys creatures without distinction between dear and not dear, causing fear to all three worlds.

verses.sanskrit: अमोघो ह्येष सर्वेषां प्राणिनाममितप्रभः । कालदण्डो मया सृष्टः पूर्वं मृत्युपुरस्कृतः ।। 7.22.43 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, this unfailing staff of Time, of immeasurable power for all living beings, was created by me long ago, with Death as its foremost aspect.

verses.sanskrit: तन्न खल्वेष ते सौम्य पात्यो रावणमूर्धनि । नह्यस्मिन्पतिते कश्चिन्मुहूर्तमपि जीवति ।। 7.22.44 ।।
verses.translation: O gentle one (Yama), this rod of yours should certainly not be cast upon Ravana's head, for indeed, no one survives even for a moment once this rod has fallen.

verses.sanskrit: यदि ह्यन्मिन्निपतिते न म्रियेतैष राक्षसः । म्रियते वा दशग्रीवस्तदाप्युभयतो ऽनृतम् ।। 7.22.45 ।।
verses.translation: If this demon (Ravana) does not die even after this rod has fallen, or if Ravana dies, then in either case, it would be a falsehood (regarding the boon of immortality).

verses.sanskrit: तन्निवर्तय लङ्केशं द्दण्डमेतं समुद्यतम् । सत्यं च मां कुरुष्वाद्य लोकांस्त्वं यद्यवेक्षसे ।। 7.22.46 ।।
verses.translation: Therefore, withdraw this uplifted rod from Ravana and make my words true now, if you indeed care for the worlds.

verses.sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तु धर्मात्मा प्रत्युवाच यमस्तदा । एष व्यावर्तितो दण्डः प्रभविष्णुर्हि नो भवान् ।। 7.22.47 ।।
verses.translation: Thus spoken to, the righteous Yama then replied, "This rod has been withdrawn, for indeed, you are our powerful lord."

verses.sanskrit: किं न्विदानीं मया शक्यं कर्तुं रणगतेन हि । न मया यद्ययं शक्यो हन्तुं वरपुरस्कृतः ।। 7.22.48 ।।
verses.translation: What indeed can I do now, having come to battle, if this one (Ravana) cannot be killed by me because he is protected by a boon?

verses.sanskrit: एष तस्मात्प्रणश्यामि दर्शनादस्य रक्षसः । इत्युक्त्वा सरथः साश्वस्तत्रैवान्तरधीयत ।। 7.22.49 ।।
verses.translation: Having said, "Therefore, I disappear from the sight of this demon," he vanished right there with his chariot and horses.

verses.sanskrit: दशग्रीवस्तु तं जित्वा नाम विश्राव्य चात्मनः । आरुह्य पुष्पकं भूयो निष्क्रान्तो यमसादनात् ।। 7.22.50 ।।
verses.translation: Dashagriva, having conquered him and proclaimed his own name, ascended the Pushpaka chariot again and departed from Yama's abode.

verses.sanskrit: स तु वैवस्वतो देवैः सह ब्रह्मपुरोगमैः । जगाम त्रिदिवं हृष्टो नारदश्च महामुनिः ।। 7.22.51 ।।
verses.translation: And Vaivasvata (Yama), delighted, went to heaven along with the gods led by Brahma, as did the great sage Narada. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे द्वाविंशः सर्गः ।। 22 ।। Thus ends the twenty-second sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
